name: Energy-Thermo
domain: Heat transfer, entropy, thermodynamic potentials, irreversibility
core_insight: Energy is conserved but quality degrades. Entropy always increases in isolated systems — this is time's arrow. Efficiency is bounded by the temperature ratio of hot and cold reservoirs. Heat diffuses; it cannot spontaneously concentrate.

key_equations.name: Second Law (Entropy)
key_equations.why: Entropy cannot decrease in isolation — the universe's ratchet that defines past from future

key_equations.name: First Law of Thermodynamics
key_equations.why: Energy bookkeeping: internal energy changes by heat in minus work done

key_equations.name: Carnot Efficiency
key_equations.why: Maximum work extractable from a heat engine — depends only on temperature ratio

seed_prompt: △≈ Which direction does entropy flow? Find the temperature gradient — that is where work lives.
